title: Aylostera donaldiana
intro: Aylostera donaldiana, also known as Donald's Living Rock, is a type of cactus that belongs to the family Cactaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Aylostera donaldiana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Aylostera donaldiana is typically used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Aylostera donaldiana is a species of cactus within the genus Aylostera. It is classified as a part of the family Cactaceae. The species is further classified as a member of the subfamily Cactoideae. Aylostera donaldiana is native to the Andes mountains in South America. The species is named after Donald E. Marsh.
